reviewed_items.reviewer_notes: Gate 5 (Spec Cross-Reference) triggered. The 01e property (from outputs/01e_PARTIAL_W2B3_1771748669.json:914-930) states 'Publishing data columns must only occur on subnets the node is subscribed to (custody/sampling)'. However, this property is incorrectly applied to block producers. In PeerDAS, block producers/proposers are REQUIRED to publish ALL data columns (all 128 columns across all subnets) to ensure data availability, regardless of their own custody subnets. The custody subnet restriction applies only to non-producers republishing received columns (distributed_blob_publishing flow). The flagged code at publish_blocks.rs:522-529 is in the HTTP API block publishing path specifically for validators proposing their own blocks. The code correctly publishes all columns (L525: compute subnet from column index, L529: publish to all computed subnets). Lines 240-244 show the node only IMPORTS columns it's subscribed to, which is correct. This is not a spec deviation but correct implementation of the block producer role. The 01e invariant is either too broadly stated or mermaid SG-010 context was misinterpreted during property generation.
reviewed_items.spec_reference: forall col: published(col, subnet) => subscribed(subnet, custody) || subscribed(subnet, sampling)
